body: {"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative and scoped. Root cause cites lefthook.yml:545-550 and cli/internal/server/perf/perf_test.go:32-37 with line precision. AC items are numbered; AC1–AC2 name explicit Test* functions; AC4 is a go test -run gate over ./cmd/...; AC5 is the lefthook pre-commit gate. Labels carry phase, area, kind, and cross-ref tags (reliability, prevention). Parent is explicit; no-deps is stated inline. No /tmp references, no chat artifacts. One deduction: PROPOSED FIX presents three mutually-exclusive implementation policies without committing to one, explicitly delegating the design decision to the executing agent ('Pick one policy and document it'). An autonomous sub-agent given only the bead body cannot deterministically choose among (a) set CGO_ENABLED=1 in the hook command, (b) fail with an actionable message, or (c) skip the race flag and run a non-race gate. This ambiguity violates P7—the bead must supply sufficient context for sub-agent execution without operator hand-curation—and risks divergent implementations or a re-prompting stall at drain time.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["In PROPOSED FIX, commit to a single policy. Recommended wording: 'Prepend CGO_ENABLED=1 to the full-tests hook command so it reads: CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race -cover ./... No fallback or skip path; if the toolchain genuinely lacks cgo the hook should fail fast with the standard Go error.' Then update AC1 to assert only that pattern, removing the 'or routes through a wrapper with equivalent CGO handling' alternative, so the agent has exactly one implementation target."],"waivers_applied":[]}
